evidence_entries.evidence_text: Retrieved candidate frames at 122.00s, 123.00s, 124.00s, 125.00s, and 126.00s near the end of the third quarter.

Across frames 122.00s-126.00s, OCR repeatedly reads the scoreboard with team labels "BRISTOL CENT." and "E. CATHOLIC," scores "52" and "40," period "3rd," and clocks counting down from about 0:02.8 to 0:00.0.

At frame 126.00s, the scoreboard overlay is located at the bottom of the image and contains the team names "BRISTOL CENT." and "E. CATHOLIC" together with the numeric scores "52" and "40" plus period/time information. This grounds that these labels and numbers belong to the same scoreboard region, but it does not explicitly state which number is paired with which team name.

inference_steps.text: The relevant scoreboard frames are near the end of the third quarter, because the retrieved frames span 122.00s to 126.00s and OCR in those frames shows the period as "3rd" with the clock decreasing from about 0:02.8 to 0:00.0.

Across those end-of-quarter frames, the scoreboard consistently shows the team labels "BRISTOL CENT." and "E. CATHOLIC" and the score pair 52 and 40. At 126.00s, spatial grounding confirms that those labels and numbers are part of the same scoreboard overlay region.
Because the clock reaches 0:00.0 while the period still reads "3rd," the end-of-third-quarter scoreboard at 126.00s shows the score pair 52 and 40.

However, the new region-grounded evidence still does not explicitly associate 52 versus 40 to a specific team name at the 126.00s frame. Without that score-to-team mapping, the trace cannot choose between the Bristol Central leading and East Catholic leading variants, and none of the listed options exactly matches a team-agnostic reading of 52 to 40.

metadata.notes: Preserved the supported end-of-third-quarter timing and the provisional score pair 52 and 40. Removed the unsupported claim that Bristol Central is definitively the team with 52, because the new region-grounded evidence confirms the shared scoreboard region but still does not explicitly map each score to each team at 126.00s.

findings.message: The trace does not produce a final answer to the multiple-choice question. It stops at an unresolved team-agnostic score pair instead of justifying one unique provided option.

The trace correctly notes that the provided text does not explicitly map score 52 versus 40 to Bristol Central versus East Catholic at the end of the third quarter. That missing score-to-team association blocks choosing between the leading-team variants.

Even if the score pair 52 and 40 is accepted, none of the listed options matches that pair. The trace identifies this mismatch but leaves the benchmark answer blank rather than resolving how the answer should map to the provided choices.

audit_report.feedback: The trace is orderly and preserves the grounded end-of-third-quarter score pair, but it remains answer-incomplete: it lacks a grounded score-to-team mapping and does not justify any one of the provided options.
missing_information: Which team has 52 and which team has 40 at the end of the third quarter
Which provided answer choice is justified by the grounded end-of-third-quarter scoreboard reading

captions.visual: A basketball game is in progress. A player in a white jersey with the number 10 receives a pass on the right wing. He takes a quick, contested shot over a defender in a maroon jersey. The ball bounces off the backboard and swishes through the net. The scoreboard shows Bristol Central with 2 points and East Catholic with 3. The score then updates to show Bristol Central with 3 points.

video_background: The scene is a large, crowded basketball arena. The stands are filled with spectators, and advertising banners are visible behind the court. The court has a large logo in the center.
storyline: The game begins with East Catholic making the first basket, giving them a 3-2 lead over Bristol Central. The score is tied 3-3 after the shot.
shooting_style: This is a standard live sports broadcast shot, following the live action of the game.

The referee raises his arm to signal the successful dunk. The score updates on the scoreboard to show Bristol Central with 5 points and East Catholic with 3.

transcript_segments.text: East Catholic will set. And a three. Buried from Glasper. Nice job by East Catholic getting back on D. Three. Got it. Another dump down to Klingin. They got a double team. He spins into it. Little running hook. And Klingin says, uh-uh. You ain't coming in here. He is not backing away. Not on my watch is the big fella.

Klingin demands it at the foul line, gets it. Spins, strong left-handed finish at the rim. That's a drop step, that's a big man move. Glasper gave up the three, drives, hangs, lefty flip. Little fancy from Damian Glasper. Yeah, he's tough. With active guards. Glasper for three, got it. He's feeling it. That's his third triple tonight. There you go. Turn the corner, the delivery and the rejection.

transcript_segments.text: There are block shots. And a stare down to Boone. Pump fake now. Riley drives baseline. Hangs. Klingin with another block. All the way in the basket. Left hand is good. What a nice move, Alan Wright. Kicks. Riley drives. And scores over the top of Klingin. Basket is good. And a foul. And a little smack talk. Smack talk, yeah.

Riley, nice job to the cutting Elliott. Reverse layup is good. Gout Klingin, glass grip for three. No good. And there it comes. The rebound and the putback for the big guy. Oh, good look. Sanchez over the top of Klingin. Basket is good. And the foul. And Klingin says, I didn't touch him. Great defense from Wright. Ahead it goes. And Riley, Sam Riley will knock it down.

transcript_segments.text: In the third period. Great look underneath and Jones off a beautiful pass. Glasper pump fake at the buzzer. Got it! Oh, a dagger from Damian Glasper at the end of period three. He wants it back. He's been out a while. There he goes up and throws it down with authority. Send it in, big fella. It's been...

Catholic up ahead it goes, Rosa ahead of the pack. And that's a nice way to break the press. A lot of hands on. Nice pass from Klingin underneath, basket by Revorick, good and a foul. Lob inside, Klingin, and an easy dunk for Donovan Klingin.
He's tied now. He needs one more to finish. And there it is. Offensive rebound. He'll go to the line. Round of applause for Donnelly Klingin.
